prose: Fukui reactivity descriptors were calculated for furan using **DFT, B3LYP/def2-TZVP** (tier standard) in the gas phase (charge 0, multiplicity 1). The calculation was a single-point energy analysis of the user-supplied geometry using the PySCF backend. Mulliken charges were computed for the neutral molecule, cation (charge +1, multiplicity 2), and anion (charge −1, multiplicity 2) to derive Fukui indices: f⁺ (electrophilic, ∂N⁺/∂ρ from cation), f⁻ (nucleophilic, ∂N⁻/∂ρ from anion), and f⁰ (dual descriptor, f⁺ − f⁻). SCF converged; charge conservation checks passed (∑f⁺ = 1.0000, ∑f⁻ = 1.0000). Integrity status: OK, trustworthy. The most electrophilic and most nucleophilic sites both localize to atom 4 (C, 1-based position #5), a β-carbon, with f⁺ = 1.924 and f⁻ = 0.162, respectively. The dual descriptor reveals β-carbons as dominantly electrophilic (values ≈ 1.76), while oxygen and hydrogens show nucleophobic or weak reactivity profiles. A visualization plot was generated at `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/furan/argo_claude-haiku-4.5/20260703-193349_furan_fukui_reactivity/agent_call_01_plot.png`.
